folio.label: 56r
folio.l: And whan they han , this blisful mayden sayne
Hom to Surrye , ben they went ful fayne
And don here nedes , as they han don ©ore
And lyuen in wele , I can sey ©ow nomore
¢ Now fel it , that theise Marchauntz stoden in gce
Of hym æat was , the Soudon of Surrie
That whan they come , fro any straunge place
He wolde of his benygne , curteisie
Make hem good cheer , and bisily aspie
Tidynges , of sondry regnes for to lere
The wondres that they , myght seen or here
¢ Amonges othere thynges specially
Theise Marchauntz han hi~ told , of Dame Custaunce
So greet noblesse , in ernest ceriously
That this Soudan hatú caugút , so gret plesaunce
To han hire figure , in his remembraunce
That al his lust , and his besy cure
Was for to loue hire , while his lif may dure
¢ îauenture , in thilke large boke
Whicú æ men clepe , the heuene , I wryten was
With sterres , whan that he his birthe toke
That he for loue , shulde han his detú , allas
For in the sterres clerere , than is glas
I wryten isis god wot , who so coude it rede
The detú of euäy man , witú outen drede
¢ In Sterres , many a wynter there bifornö
Was wretyn the detú , of Ector Achilles
Of pompey Iulyus , er they were bornö
The strif of Thebes , and of Hercules
Of Sampsou~ , Turnus , and of Socrates
The detú , but mennes wyttes ben so dulle
That no wygút can wel rede it , at the fulle
¢ This Soudan , for his pryue counseil sent´
And shortly of this mater , for to pace
He hatú to hem declared , his entent
And seide he~ certeyn , but he mygút han grace
To haue Custaunce , w inne a litel space
He nas but dede , and charged hem in hye
To shapen for his lyf , som remedye

note.notetxt: 112the priking in the right margin pointed out before -RE- has a small cross in dry point over it.ODR

folio.l: ¢ Diuäse men , diuäse thynges seiden
They Argumentz cast , vp and doun
Many a subtile resou~ , fortú they leiden
They speken of magyk , and of abusione
But finally , as in conclusione
